title: Analytics - Software Language Change
scenario: DFU analytics event with language change does not display all expected Firebase elements (success/failure, device name, firmware versions, language IDs, update progress, time, error description)
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Navigate to the Home screen and ensure a supported Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
3. Tap on the connected [Device Name] to open Device Settings.
4. Initiate a Device Firmware Update (DFU) that includes a software language change (e.g., via a firmware package that updates the device's language).
5. Wait for the DFU process to complete, monitoring the update progress for each component (headset, charge case, charge stand if applicable).
6. After the update finishes, verify the device language has changed to the target language.
7. Open Firebase Analytics and navigate to the event page for the DFU-related analytics event.
8. Check that the event page displays default elements: graph, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version/build.

9. Verify the event includes custom parameters: success/failure status, device name, firmware update status, current and target versions for headset/charge case/charge stand, current and target language IDs, target language name, update progress for each component, update time in seconds, and error description (if applicable).
10. Wait a few hours after triggering the language change, then re-check the Firebase Analytics event count to confirm the event count for related elements has increased.
expected: The Firebase Analytics event page displays all default elements (graph, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version/build) and custom parameters (success/failure status, device name, firmware update status, current and target versions for headset/charge case/charge stand, current and target language IDs, target language name, update progress for each component, update time in seconds, error description). After a few hours, the event count for related elements increases.
